name: Key Lime Pie (Low Fat)
recipeIngredient: 2 large egg whites at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
To Make Crust: Preheat oven to 350°F. Spray a 9-inch pie plate with nonstick cooking spray. In a medium-sized bowl, beat the egg white lightly with a fork until frothy. Add graham cracker crumbs, butter, and oil; blend until thoroughly combined. Press mixture evenly onto bottom and sides of pie plate. Bake for 10 minutes or until lightly browned (small cracks are acceptable). Cool on a rack.
To Make Filling: In a medium-sized metal bowl, whisk together milk, yogurt, lime juice, and zest. In a small saucepan, sprinkle gelatin over 2 tablespoons cold water. Let soften for 1 minute. Heat over low heat, stirring, until the gelatin is dissolved. Whisk into the lime mixture. Set bowl over larger bowl of ice water; stir occasionally until it begins to thicken, about 15-20 minutes. Turn filling into pie shell and chill in refrigerator while preparing meringue.
To Make Meringue: Preheat broiler. In a small saucepan, combine sugar with 1/2 cup water. Bring to boil, stirring occasionally. Cook over medium-high heat until syrup reaches 240°F and is at soft-ball stage (a bit of syrup dropped into ice water forms a pliable ball), about 5 minutes. Remove from heat; set aside. In a large mixing bowl, beat egg whites on medium speed until frothy. Add cream of tartar and beat on high speed until soft peaks form. Return syrup to heat until boils. Gradually pour hot syrup into egg whites without pouring directly onto beaters; beat constantly. Continue beating until egg whites are cool and very stiff, about 5 minutes. Blend in vanilla.
Remove pie from refrigerator and spread meringue over top, sealing to the edge of crust and swirling roughly on top. Place under broiler for 1-2 minutes or until lightly browned. Let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until filling has set, about 5-8 hours. Garnish with fresh lime slices just before serving.
